name: Homefirst Housing Resource Services, INC.
description_short: HUD-approved housing counseling agency in Macon, GA offering homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and financial literacy services since 2002.
description_long:
HomeFirst Housing Resource Services, Inc. has operated as a non-profit housing counseling agency in Macon-Bibb and Middle Georgia since 2002, with HUD approval as a certified housing counselor. The organization's stated mission is to provide homeownership education, counseling, financial literacy, and housing development services to address local housing needs and increase homeownership rates in the region. Their services address the full homeownership lifecycle, from initial education for first-time buyers through pre-purchase coaching to foreclosure intervention for homeowners in distress.

The agency offers three primary service lines: HomeBuyer Education (foundational homeownership knowledge), Pre-Purchasing Coaching (individualized guidance for buyers), and Foreclosure Intervention (assistance for those behind on mortgage payments or facing foreclosure). They serve clients throughout Macon-Bibb, Middle Georgia, and surrounding counties. Historically, HomeFirst has funded operations through Community Development Block Grants (CDBG) and other funding sources, enabling them to provide services at no cost to clients.

What distinguishes HomeFirst is its HUD-approved status, which signifies compliance with federal housing counseling standards and training requirements. The organization explicitly positions itself as a non-profit resource center, meaning counselors are not incentivized to sell financial products or services. Their long operational history since 2002 indicates sustained community presence and established relationships with local housing stakeholders.

A significant caveat emerged in 2025: HomeFirst announced anticipated decreases in federal funding and has implemented a fee structure for services and reduced office/staff hours effective immediately. The organization transparently communicated that funding shifts will impact service availability going forward. Prospective clients should contact the agency directly to understand current fee schedules and operating hours, as this represents a material change from the non-profit's historical free-service model.
pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency meeting federal certification standards
Long operational history since 2002 providing credibility and community relationships
Serves full homeownership lifecycle: education, purchase coaching, and foreclosure prevention
Explicitly non-profit model with no incentive to sell financial products
CDBG-funded organization historically providing services at no cost
Local presence in Macon-Bibb and Middle Georgia with established regional expertise
Transparent communication about funding changes and service impacts
cons: Announced fee structure implementation in 2025-2026 ending historical free-service model
Reduced office and staff hours effective immediately, limiting accessibility
Service availability contingent on uncertain federal funding priorities
Limited geographic service area (Macon-Bibb and Middle Georgia counties only)
No online services or remote counseling mentioned on website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Macon-Bibb or Middle Georgia seeking education and pre-purchase guidance
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage payment delinquency in their service area
Consumers seeking HUD-certified counseling from a non-profit with no product-sales incentive
diagnosis: HomeFirst is best suited for homebuyers or distressed homeowners in Macon-Bibb and Middle Georgia seeking HUD-certified, non-profit counseling. Critical caveat: the organization has shifted to a fee-based model with reduced hours starting in 2025 due to federal funding decreases, so prospective clients must contact them directly for current service costs and availability before engagement.
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
